records.ety: [OE. pris, OF. pris, F. prix, L. pretium; cf. Gr. <?/ I sell <?/ to buy, Skr. pa<?/ to buy, OI. renim I sell. Cf. Appreciate, Depreciate, Interpret, Praise, n. & v., Precious, Prize.]

defs: The sum or amount of money at which a thing is valued, or the value which a seller sets on his goods in market; that for which something is bought or sold, or offered for sale; equivalent in money or other means of exchange; current value or rate paid or demanded in market or in barter; cost.
quotes: We can afford no more at such a price.
Her price is far above rubies.
New treasures still, of countless price.

defs: Reward; recompense; as, the price of industry.
quotes: 'T is the price of toil, The knave deserves it when he tills the soil.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Priced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Pricing.]

quotes: With thine own blood to price his blood.

defs: To set a price on; to value. See Prize.
To ask the price of; as, to price eggs.
